subject: Re: How is tomostar wrpAxisAngle generated?
body:
hi folks,

I am encountering a similar issue with one of the datasets. 

The transformation from .xf is not getting properly transferred to WARP. 
Transformations from .xf using IMOD's tilt_com is producing a nice sharp 
reconstruction. But when using the same .xf for importing into WARP, the 
reconstruction and its related CTF estimation are completely off. It has 
never happened to me with any other dataset, but only this one. 

Joe, were you able to resolve this issue? 

Cheers,
Digvijay

On Wednesday, August 9, 2023 at 12:32:23 AM UTC-7 joe dobbs wrote:

> I was able to trick warp into calculating the correct angle by deleting a 
> couple of the decimal values from the first 4 columns of the xf, but my 
> tomograms still look terrible. Clearly the alignments are not being 
> properly used in warp, somehow, but I cannot figure out how this can be the 
> case. See attached for a comparison, it's not the usual warp vs. imod 
> differences but the actual alignments don't seem to be applied properly 
> when reconstructing in warp.
>
> Does anyone know why this might be the case? It's very bizarre that this 
> has only occurred in this dataset for me and I'm at a loss for what's going 
> on.
>
> On Monday, July 3, 2023 at 12:27:13 AM UTC+2 joe dobbs wrote:
>
>> To be more precise, the exact behavior is:
>> tilt axis angle = 85.2 degrees
>> calculated = 94.8 degrees
>>
>> (180-94.8 = 85.2)
>> On Monday, July 3, 2023 at 12:12:19 AM UTC+2 joe dobbs wrote:
>>
>>> Dear Alister,
>>>
>>> Thanks for the information and suggestions. That's exactly the case, the 
>>> calculated angle differs by exactly 180 degrees. Any tips for tricking warp 
>>> into getting the right one, or do you think it would be a bug that would 
>>> need more of a "software update" kind of fix?
>>>
>>> Thanks again,
>>> Joe
>>>
>>> On Monday, July 3, 2023 at 12:04:47 AM UTC+2 alist...@gmail.com wrote:
>>>
>>>> Hi Joe,
>>>>
>>>> Does anyone know how wrpAxisAngle in the tomostar file is generated? 
>>>> From the github it looks like some transformation of the offset 
>>>> coordinates. I suppose we would expect it to come out to be the same as the 
>>>> tilt axis offset?
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> wrpAxisAngle is the orientation of the tilt-axis within the tilt 
>>>> images, it’s calculated here 
>>>> <https://github.com/cramerlab/warp/blob/21e804c6b362154dae6177f381e48198e599df2c/Warp/Controls/TaskDialogs/Tomo/DialogTomoImportImod.xaml.cs#L268-L296> from 
>>>> the parts of the IMOD xf data which encode the 2D image rotation (first 
>>>> four columns).
>>>>
>>>> I have some data where the warp reconstructions differ substantially 
>>>> from reconstructions made in etomo (with the same xf) and they have a 
>>>> different wrpAxisAngle in the tomostars than the known tilt axis angle, 
>>>> where I have seen in the past they've been the same, and I wonder if this 
>>>> could be where my reconstructions are going wrong.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> I’m assuming the number you have is your actual tilt axis angle +- ~180 
>>>> degrees? The angle is calculated in Warp by decomposing a 3x3 rotation 
>>>> matrix into Euler angles and taking one of those angles - because there can 
>>>> be multiple valid sets of Euler angles for a given matrix it’s likely this 
>>>> is where the error is occurring. If this is the case it’s a bug and would 
>>>> need to be fixed within Warp.
>>>>
>>>> Cheers,
>>>>
>>>> Alister
>>>>  
>>>>
>>>> On 2 Jul 2023, at 16:53, joe dobbs <dobbsj...@gmail.com> wrote:
>>>>
>>>> Hello,
>>>>
>>>> Does anyone know how wrpAxisAngle in the tomostar file is generated? 
>>>> From the github it looks like some transformation of the offset 
>>>> coordinates. I suppose we would expect it to come out to be the same as the 
>>>> tilt axis offset?
>>>>
>>>> I have some data where the warp reconstructions differ substantially 
>>>> from reconstructions made in etomo (with the same xf) and they have a 
>>>> different wrpAxisAngle in the tomostars than the known tilt axis angle, 
>>>> where I have seen in the past they've been the same, and I wonder if this 
>>>> could be where my reconstructions are going wrong.
>>>>
>>>>
>>>> Best,
>>>> Joe.
>>>>
>>>> --

>>>> .
>>>>
>>>>
